steps.title: Use the middle value
steps.content: Each data set has $9$ values, so the median is the $5$th value. A set with large high values can have a mean greater than its median because those values pull the average upward.

steps.title: Check the right-skewed set
steps.content: For the doubling data set, the median is $48$. Its mean is $$ \frac{3+6+12+24+48+96+192+384+768}{9}=170\frac{1}{3} $$ Since $170\frac{1}{3}>48$, its mean is greater than its median. The answer is C.
